REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, CROSS-COMPLAINANT KAMAN LIUS NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE ORDER DISMISSING CROSS-COMPLAINT ON 2023-03-27 IS DENIED. HOWEVER, ON ITS OWN MOTION, THE COURT VACATES ITS MARCH 27, 2023 RULING DISMISSING THE LIU'S CROSS-COMPLAINT, AND REINSTATES THE CROSS-COMPLAINT AS TO TONY FU, CRYSTAL LEI, AND BRYANT FU. IN THEIR MOTION TO DISMISS THE CROSS-COMPLAINT, CRYSTAL LEI AND BRYANT FU, CLAIMED THAT THE BANKRUPTCY COURT HAD ISSUED A DISCHARGE ORDER ON MARCH 29, 2019 PROHIBITING THE CONTINUED PROSECUTION OF LIU'S CROSS-COMPLAINT AGAINST TONY FU. IT HAS COME TO THE COURT'S ATTENTION THAT A SUBSEQUENT ORDER, DATED SEPTEMBER 25, 2020, STATES THE EXACT OPPOSITE: THAT LIU'S CROSS-CLAIMS, AS POTENTIAL SET-OFFS, DO NOT VIOLATE THE DISCHARGE INJUNCTION. (FINAL ORDER RE: DISCHARGED DEBTOR'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS, CASE NO. 17-41205CN, PP. 5-7.) WITH RESPECT TO THE BANKRUPTCY STAY FILED APRIL 26, 2017, IT APPLIED AS TO ALL CROSS-DEFENDANTS NOT JUST THE DEBTOR, IN THIS CASE, TONY FU. THE AUTOMATIC STAY APPLIES TO A CREDITOR'S FRAUDULENT TRANSFER ACTION AGAINST A TRANSFEREE FROM THE DEBTOR TO THE EXTENT THE ACTION SEEKS SATISFACTION OF A CLAIM FROM THE DEBTOR. (IN RE KOEBERER (9TH CIR. BAP 2021) 632 BR 680, 687-688.) THAT IS EXACTLY WHAT LIU'S CROSS-COMPLAINT IS ALL ABOUT-FRAUDULENT TRANSFERS FROM TONY FU TO HIS WIFE CRYSTAL LEI AND HIS SON BRYANT FU. (CROSS-COMPLAINT, 11-13.) BASED ON THE MARCH 29, 2019 DISCHARGE ORDER (WHICH THE COURT NEVER RECEIVED A COPY OF), THE 5-YEAR DEADLINE EXPIRES ON SEPTEMBER 14, 2024. THE COURT USED THE MARCH 29, 2019 DATE, IT BEING THE MOST CONSERVATIVE APPROACH TO ASCERTAIN THE 5-YEAR DEADLINE. NONETHELESS, THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO OBTAIN AND FILE THE RELEVANT DOCUMENT(S) THIS COURT NEEDS TO DETERMINE THE 5-YEAR STATUTORY DEADLINE WITHIN 30 DAYS OF TODAY'S HEARING. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: L. TAUALA ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

The undisputed material facts as to the dates of the transfers alleged in the operative Cross-Complaints (the latest in 2003) establish that as a matter of law at the time of the filing of the Cross-Complaint in 2017 the right of action was extinguished by the statute of repose as a cause of action that has not been filed "within seven years after the transfer was made" no matter whether the claim is "brought under the UFTA or otherwise" is barred. PGA West Residential Assn., Inc. v. Hulven Internat., Inc., (2017) 14 Cal.App.5th 156. The Cross-Complaint, as currently alleged, does not include any factual allegations as to any transfers that occurred within 7 years of the filing of the cross-complaint. Request for continuance is denied. There is no indication that discovery will be relevant to the issue of the statute of repose.

Cross-Complainant Kaman Lius Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order Dismissing Cross-Complaint On 2023-03-27
Off Calendar May-17-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Bryant Fu (415)221-0969 Cross defendant appearing in pro per.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KAMAN LIU Cross-Complainant Kaman Lius Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order Dismissing Cross-Complaint On 2023-03-27 is DENIED. However, on its own motion, the Court vacates its March 27, 2023 ruling dismissing the Liu's Cross-Complaint, and reinstates the cross-complaint as to Tony Fu, Crystal Lei, and Bryant Fu. In their Motion to Dismiss the Cross-Complaint, Crystal Lei and Bryant Fu, claimed that the Bankruptcy Court had issued a Discharge Order on March 29, 2019 prohibiting the continued prosecution of Liu's Cross-Complaint against Tony Fu. It has come to the Court's attention that a subsequent Order, dated September 25, 2020, states the exact opposite: that Liu's cross-claims, as potential set-offs, do not violate the discharge injunction. (Final Order Re: Discharged Debtor's Motion for Sanctions, Case No. 17-41205CN, pp. 5-7.)
With respect to the Bankruptcy Stay filed April 26, 2017, it applied as to all cross-defendants not just the debtor, in this case, Tony Fu. The automatic stay applies to a creditor's fraudulent transfer action against a transferee from the debtor to the extent the action seeks satisfaction of a claim from the debtor. (In re Koeberer (9th Cir. BAP 2021) 632 BR 680, 687-688.) That is exactly what Liu's cross-complaint is all about-fraudulent transfers from Tony Fu to his wife Crystal Lei and his son Bryant Fu. (Cross-complaint, 11-13.)
Based on the March 29, 2019 Discharge Order (which the Court never received a copy of), the 5-year deadline expires on September 14, 2024. The Court used the March 29, 2019 date, it being the most conservative approach to ascertain the 5-year deadline. Nonetheless, the parties are ordered to obtain and file the relevant document(s) this Court needs to determine the 5-year statutory deadline within 30 days of today's hearing.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Tauala ; Not Reported. (D501)

Off Calendar Aug-03-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF TONY FU's Motion For Permission To File First Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff Tony Fu's motion for permission to file first amended complaint is granted. There is a strong policy in favor of liberal allowance of amendments to pleadings. (Mesler v. Bragg Mgmt. Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 290, 296.) If Defendants wish to challenge the sufficiency of the new causes of action on the ground that they are uncertain or time-barred, the appropriate mechanism for doing so is by way of a demurrer or dispositive motion. (See California Casualty Gen. Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 ["the preferable practice would be to permit the amendment and allow the parties to test its legal sufficiency by demurrer, motion for judgment on the pleadings or other appropriate proceedings."].) There is no showing that Defendants would be prejudiced by the amendment.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
